title: World regions according to the World Health Organization
caption:
World regions according to the World Health Organization

This choropleth world map displays the geographic classification of countries into six distinct regions as defined by the World Health Organization. Each region is assigned a unique color to distinguish it from the others. The Americas, covering North, Central, and South America along with the Caribbean, are shaded in orange. The entire African continent, including Madagascar, is colored purple. The Eastern Mediterranean region, which includes countries in North Africa, the Middle East, and parts of Central Asia, appears in a tan or light brown shade. Europe is depicted in dark blue, encompassing the continent from the Atlantic to the Ural Mountains. The South-East Asia region, including India and Indonesia, is shown in green. The Western Pacific region, covering East Asia, Australia, and the Pacific Islands, is colored teal. Greenland is marked with diagonal hatching, indicating it is categorized as having no data within this specific regional framework. The map provides a clear visual segmentation of the globe based on these administrative groupings, with distinct boundaries separating the colored zones.
